title: Galactic Solutions Industries

{{Quote|We stay neutral, so you don't have to.|GSI motto|<ref name="Developer" />}}
'''Galactic Solutions Industries''' (GSI) was a weapons manufacturing company that was founded by inventor and business tycoon [[Addalar Hyland]] as the [[Galactic War]] broke out between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]]. A neutral corporation, GSI dealt weapons to both governments, and frequently hired various individuals to aid its many research divisions in their efforts to develop new weapons technologies and military gear. The company was still in business during the [[New Sith Wars]], a conflict between the Republic and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] over two thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after GSI's founding, and the company's [[Disruptor pistol/Legends|disruptor pistols]] were favorites of a [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] named [[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]] in the Sith [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]].

==History==
Galactic Solutions Industries was a weapons-manufacturing company that was founded by [[Addalar Hyland]], a well-known inventor and business tycoon,<ref name="Codex" /> around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3642 BBY]]<ref name="Time" /> as the [[Galactic War]] broke out between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]]. Based in the [[Galactic Solutions Industries Headquarters|GSI Headquarters]] on the [[Promenade]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], GSI soon became a popular source of weaponry, and the company's first product—the [[GSI-B4 blaster pistol|GSI-B4]] [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]—was praised for its impressive firepower compared to its small size as an easily concealed holdout weapon. As war resumed, GSI remained neutral and did business with both the Republic and the Empire, and it often recruited various combatants on [[Planet/Legends|planets]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to aid in the company's research.<ref name="Codex" /> GSI maintained a number of weapons research divisions, including their [[Animal Weaponization Division|Animal Weaponization]],<ref name="Krayt" /> [[Biodesign Division|Biodesign]],<ref name="Makrin" /> [[Metallurgical Division|Metallurgical]],<ref name="Rare Metals" /> [[Natural Weapons Division|Natural Weapons]],<ref name="Weather Tower" /> [[Starfighter Division|Starfighter]],<ref name="Hazardous Materials" /> and [[Superweapons Division]]s.<ref name="Data Recovery" /> Employees of the company typically wore GSI's colors of white, orange, and light blue.<ref name="ROTHC" />

On the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], GSI's Natural Weapons Division sought to acquire technology in Alderaan's weather towers that could aid in the construction of a "Weather Dominator" prototype, and because the rights to the technology were in dispute, the company recruited various individuals to acquire scans of the towers so that they could reverse-engineer the technology.<ref name="Weather Tower" /> GSI also fitted several [[red thranta]]s, flying creatures native to Alderaan, with GSI technology to gather data on troop deployments in the ongoing [[Alderaan Civil War]], and recruited various individuals to retrieve the data.<ref name="Alderaan Data Recovery">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Surveillance Data Recovery|Alderaan}}</ref> The independent intelligence operative known as [[The Shroud (Human)|the Shroud]] hired a [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] to steal GSI weapons technology from the company's [[Memory bank/Legends|databanks]], and the company traced the perpetrator to one of the [[Great Houses of Alderaan]]'s communications networks, so GSI hired individuals to monitor the networks in order to locate and defeat the thief. The company's [[Recovery Division]] then reacquired the technology from the [[Death/Legends|dead]] thief.<ref name="Saboteur at Large">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsiact|Saboteur at Large|Alderaan}}</ref>

Upon learning that [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] had buried radioactive waste on Alderaan years earlier instead of following proper disposal protocols in order to save money, GSI initiated orbital scans of the planet to locate the waste as it was a potential fuel source. When the company learned that the native [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] had built nests over the dump sites, GSI hired several individuals to retrieve the waste barrels from the area, and the company's [[Starfighter Division]] made use of the barrels to power new designs.<ref name="Hazardous Materials" /> The Killiks' hive mind also drew the interest of GSI, as the hive mind produced hybrid weapons using the collective knowledge of dozens of individuals from various civilizations. As a result, the company hired several individuals to retrieve those weapons from where they were buried underneath Killik ruins on Alderaan.<ref name="Weapons Testing">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Weapons Testing|Alderaan}}</ref>

[[File:GSI_speeder.jpg|thumb|left|250px|GSI Headquarters on Nar Shaddaa]]
The ice planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] possessed a unique combination of electromagnetic fields and gravitational pull that often caused [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to crash on its surface, and GSI's Natural Weapons Division employed outside aid in examining the wreckage across the planet in hopes of reverse-engineering the circumstances in which the ships crashed.<ref name="Crash Site">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Crash Site Readings|Hoth}}</ref> The [[White Maw]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] stole sensitive research from GSI's Superweapons Division, but the pirates crashed on their return to Hoth, leading GSI to hire several individuals to retrieve the data from the crash site.<ref name="Data Recovery" /> The crash site of the ''[[Ambria's Fury]]'' drew the company's attention when it was discovered that leaking radiation had mutated the ship's [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] into what they believed could be a material useful in the construction of thermonuclear weaponry, and GSI hired a number of individuals to retrieve samples from the heavily irradiated ship.<ref name="Irradiated">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Irradiated Durasteel Samples|Hoth}}</ref>

A starship of unknown origin that was discovered frozen underneath ice spires drew the attention of the company, which hired individuals to recover components of the vessel for their Starfighter Division.<ref name="Debris">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Unidentified Ship Debris|Hoth}}</ref> GSI also sought to acquire samples of [[Embersteel/Legends|embersteel]], a metallic compound that formed when metallic ores were melted by lava vents, warped by Hoth's electromagnetic fields, and then instantly cooled by Hoth's icy temperatures. The company therefore hired a number of individuals to search near the active mini-volcanoes that lined the floor of the [[Glacial Fissure]] on Hoth, as they suspected embersteel could be useful in the construction of [[Plasma cannon/Legends|plasma cannons]].<ref name="Embersteel">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Embersteel Samples|Hoth}}</ref>

When a meteoroid broke up over the desert planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], GSI's orbital sensors detected the energy signature of a particularly dense and rare variant of the material [[phobium]], leading the company to hire individuals to locate and scan the fragments in order to determine whether the fragments could be useful in the construction of fuel cells.<ref name="Space Debris">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Space Debris Removal|Tatooine}}</ref> A shipment of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] repair [[Droid/Legends|droids]] was lost on Tatooine when it was scavenged by the local [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]], and because of sandstorm activity in the area, the company hired individuals to pinpoint Jawa [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawlers]] so that GSI could send retrieval units.<ref name="Scavenged Prototypes">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Scavenged Prototypes|Tatooine}}</ref> GSI's Animal Weaponization Division sought outside help in recovering eggs from Tatooine's native [[Krayt dragon/Legends|krayt dragon]], the largest and most feared creature in Tatooine's entire ecosystem, so that they could potentially clone the creatures and conduct tests in laboratory settings. The company advised the searchers to look for [[penakin]] larvae, which fed on the nutrients from krayt dragon egg fossils.<ref name="Krayt" />

GSI also sought to develop portable [[Moisture vaporator/Legends|moisture vaporators]] for use in military settings, and they therefore hired various individuals to take detailed scans of the the scaled-down vaporators used by Tatooine's settlers in cliff and mesa settlements across the planet.<ref name="Vaporators">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Examine Vaporators|Tatooine}}</ref> However, one of GSI's [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] prototypes was stolen by a thief on Tatooine, and because the thief was using [[subsurface scrambler]]s to hide the droid's location, GSI sought outside aid in locating and destroying the droid so that the company's technology was not copied.<ref name="Rogue Droid">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsiact|Rogue Droid|Tatooine}}</ref>

When the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] [[Conquest of Makeb|conquered]] the remote world of [[Makeb]] in [[3638 BBY]], GSI began investigating the planet for potential research opportunities, and the company hired various individuals to scan the advanced transmission towers around the [[Sanctuary research complex]] on Makeb so that they could replicate the technology.<ref name="Encryption">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Encryption Coding|Makeb}}</ref> GSI's [[Metallurgical Division]] sought to procure samples of the metal used in the Hutt Cartel's advanced [[isotope-5 droid]]s, an alloy that consisted of rare metals only found in the caverns of Makeb's volcanic mesas. The company accordingly hired individuals to recover samples of the metals from the caverns so that they could use the alloy for their own battle droids and weapons.<ref name="Rare Metals" /> GSI also sought to acquire the schematics for the isotope-5 droids, leading them to hire various individuals to retrieve the schematics from Cartel-allied mercenaries on Makeb.<ref name="Schematics">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsiact|Prototype Schematics|Makeb}}</ref>

Makeb's proclivity towards severe [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]] saw [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] try to create a system of subterranean vibration emitters that were intended to cancel out seismic tremors, and though the project failed, GSI believed that they could turn the devices into weapons that would cause groundquakes, so the company hired various individuals to locate and excavate the emitters.<ref name="Groundquake">{{TORcite|exp=rothc|type=gsireq|Groundquake Technology|Makeb}}</ref> Makeb's [[makrin creeper]] interested GSI's Biodesign Division, which recruited several individuals to retrieve makrin [[protobark]]—the casings in which the tree-like makrin develops underground—so that they could research the creation of damage-resistant wilderness gear.<ref name="Makrin" /> Those individuals who completed multiple assignments for GSI were qualified for the company's [[Valued Partner Initiative]], which rewarded individuals with discounts and access to special items.<ref name="ROTHC" /> The company still existed several thousand years later by the end of the [[New Sith Wars]], a conflict between the Republic and various [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] factions.<ref name="PoD" />

==Products==
[[File:GSI_HQ.jpg|thumb|right|240px|A number of GSI products on display at the company's headquarters]]
Galactic Solutions Industries was primarily a weapons manufacturer, and its first product was the critically praised GSI-B4 [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol.<ref name="Codex" /> The company also branched out into a wide variety of weapon technologies, from biological weapons<ref name="Krayt" /> and battle droids<ref name="Rogue Droid" /> to starfighters<ref name="Hazardous Materials" /> and superweapons,<ref name="Data Recovery" /> and GSI also developed and manufactured military support technology such as advanced fuel cells<ref name="Vaporators" /> and portable vaporators.<ref name="Vaporators" /> By the time of the New Sith Wars, two of GSI's products were the [[GSI-21D]]<ref name="PoD" /> and [[GSI-24D disruptor pistol|GSI-24D]]<ref name="DoE">''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''</ref> [[Disruptor pistol/Legends|disruptor pistols]]. [[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]], a [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]] in the [[Gloom Walkers]], a [[unit]] in the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]' [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Army/Legends|army]], preferred to use Galactic Solutions Industries products.<ref name="PoD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Galactic Solutions Industries was first mentioned in the novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', written by [[Drew Karpyshyn]] and released on [[September 26]], [[2006]]. The company was later featured in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]'', the [[2013]] Digital Expansion to the [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', where GSI issues missions on Makeb, Alderaan, Tatooine, and Hoth for players who possess [[EV2 discreet reconnaissance macrobinoculars]] and [[Seeker Droid]]s.<ref name="ROTHC" />
